params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears, scale of 5, showing Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.

steps.text: In Warmup, you read graphs with different scales. Now let's use graphs to solve problems. Here's data about students' favorite lunch foods.
First, a quick check on something you learned before.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.

steps.text: How many more students chose pizza than salad? Remember what 'how many more' tells us to do.
How many more students chose pizza than salad?

validator.description: Student answered 35, correct. Pizza 45 minus Salad 10 equals 35.

steps.text: 35 more. 'How many more' means compare, and compare means subtract. Pizza is 45, salad is 10, so 45 minus 10 is 35.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Pizza and Salad bars highlighted. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.
Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.

steps.text: Good. 'How many more' means compare, and compare means subtract. You've got that. Now here's something new: What if the problem asks about more than one category?

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.

steps.text: Remember this question from Warmup? You noticed it needed more than one step. Now let's figure out how to solve it.
First, let's find the categories we need. Which categories does the question ask about?
Which categories do we need? Select all of the needed categories.

validator.description: Student selected Pizza, Tacos, and Salad. Correct.

steps.text: Those three. Notice we don't need burgers. The question doesn't ask about burgers.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Pizza, Tacos, and Salad bars highlighted. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.

validator.description: Branch 2: Student selected only correct categories, but not all three

steps.text: You found some of the categories we need. The question asks about pizza, tacos, and salad. Are you missing any?

validator.description: Branch 3: Student selected only incorrect categories

steps.text: Let's think about what the question asks. It asks about pizza, tacos, and salad, not other foods. Which categories match those three?

validator.description: Branch 4: Student selected mix of correct and incorrect categories

steps.text: You got some of the right categories. But one of your choices doesn't fit. The question asks only about pizza, tacos, and salad.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Pizza, Tacos, and Salad bars highlight one by one to demonstrate the correct selections

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. The question asks about pizza, tacos, and salad. Those are the three categories we need. We don't need burgers because the question doesn't ask about them.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Multi-select tool active.

steps.text: Let me show you how I solve this. First, I ask myself: What should I find first?
The question says 'tacos and salad together.' That word 'together' tells me to add those first. Let me find that total.

params.description: Animation shows 25 + 10 = 35 next to highlighted Tacos and Salad bars

steps.text: Tacos is 25, salad is 10. Combined, that's 35.
Now I can compare. How many more is pizza than that total of 35?

params.description: Shows 45 compared to 35, with difference calculation 45 - 35 = 10

steps.text: Pizza is 45 while tacos and salad combined is 35. 45 minus 35 is 10. Pizza is 10 more than tacos and salad combined.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Pizza bar highlighted. Calculation shows 45 - 35 = 10. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.

params.description: Strategy statement appears on screen: Find the combined total first, then compare.

steps.text: Here's the strategy: When you see 'combined' or 'together' or 'altogether,' find the total of those categories first. Then compare to the other category.
Find the combined total first, then compare. That's the key to these problems. What do we find first when we see 'combined'?
What do we find first when we see 'combined'?
Find the total by adding. That's always our first step when we see 'combined.'

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.
Strategy statement displayed: Find the combined total first, then compare.
Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15.
Strategy statement displayed: Find the combined total first, then compare.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears, scale of 10, showing Favorite Ways to Roll. Skateboards 15, Rollerblades 10, Bikes 65, Scooters 30.

steps.text: Your turn. Here's a new graph.
This problem needs two steps. What should we find first?
What should we find first?

validator.description: Student selected 'Add scooters and skateboards', correct.

steps.text: Right. Scooters and skateboards combined. Let's find that total.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale of 10. Skateboards 15, Rollerblades 10, Bikes 65, Scooters 30.
Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale of 10. Skateboards 15, Rollerblades 10, Bikes 65, Scooters 30. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Shows 30 + 15 = 45 next to highlighted Scooters and Skateboards bars

steps.text: Scooters and skateboards combined is 45. Now we compare. How many more is bikes than 45?
How many more is bikes than 45?

validator.description: Student answered 20, correct. Bikes 65 minus combined 45 equals 20.

params.description: Shows 65 - 45 = 20, comparing Bikes bar to combined total

steps.text: 20 more. Bikes is 65, combined is 45, so 65 minus 45 is 20. You used the strategy: find the combined total first, then compare.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Bikes bar highlighted. Calculation shows 65 - 45 = 20. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale of 10. Skateboards 15, Rollerblades 10, Bikes 65, Scooters 30.
Vertical bar graph. Scooters and Skateboards bars highlighted. Combined calculation 30 + 15 = 45 displayed. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale of 10. Skateboards 15, Rollerblades 10, Bikes 65, Scooters 30.

steps.text: You're getting it. Find the combined total first, then compare. Let's practice more, and I'll give you less help each time.

params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears, scale of 5, showing Fruit Stand Sales. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25.

steps.text: Here's another one. What should we find first?
What should we find first?

validator.description: Student selected 'Add oranges and grapes', correct.

steps.text: Right. Oranges and grapes combined. Find that total.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25.
Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25. Multiple choice tool active.

steps.text: Oranges is 25. Grapes is 15. What's the combined total?
How many oranges and grapes combined?

validator.description: Student answered 40, correct. Oranges 25 plus Grapes 15 equals 40.

params.description: Shows 25 + 15 = 40 next to highlighted Oranges and Grapes bars

steps.text: 40 combined.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Oranges and Grapes bars highlighted. Calculation shows 25 + 15 = 40. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25.
Horizontal bar graph. Oranges and Grapes bars highlighted. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25. Multiple choice tool active.

steps.text: Oranges and grapes combined is 40. Apples is 45. Now find how many fewer.
How many fewer oranges and grapes combined than apples?

validator.description: Student answered 5, correct. Apples 45 minus combined 40 equals 5.

params.description: Shows 45 - 40 = 5, comparing Apples bar to combined total of 40

steps.text: 5 fewer. You found the combined total first, then compared.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Apples bar highlighted. Calculation shows 45 - 40 = 5. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25.
Horizontal bar graph. Apples bar highlighted. Combined total 40 displayed. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes 15, Apples 45, Bananas 30, Oranges 25. Multiple choice tool active.

steps.text: One more. Think about what to do first.
What should we find first?

validator.description: Student selected 'Add fish and birds', correct.

steps.text: Fish and birds combined. Find that total.

steps.text: Fish is 30. Birds is 25. What's the combined total of fish and birds?
How many fish and birds combined?

steps.text: 55 combined.

steps.text: Fish and birds combined is 55. Now find how many more dogs there are.
How many more dogs than fish and birds combined?

validator.description: Student answered 10, correct. Dogs 65 minus combined 55 equals 10.

params.description: Shows 65 - 55 = 10, comparing Dogs bar to combined total of 55

steps.text: 10 more dogs. You found the combined total first, then compared.

steps.text: How about this one? How many more dogs are there than cats?
How many more dogs are there than cats?

validator.description: Student answered 25, correct. Dogs 65 minus Cats 40 equals 25.

steps.text: 25 more dogs than cats. This one was just one step. No 'combined,' so straight to comparing.

steps.text: Total green and yellow first.
